task:
Draft a new governance ADR that opens the `oz-autonomy` priority's write-side autonomy. This is the priority's mandatory FIRST deliverable: per its Objective, no build atom lands until this ADR is founder-approved. You are drafting it as a PROPOSED decision for the founder to approve — you are NOT approving it.

FILE: create `cocoder/decisions/0040-oz-write-side-autonomy.md` (0040 is the next free ADR number; ticket 0040 is unrelated). Match the exact format of recent ADRs — read `cocoder/decisions/0036-oscar-deb-repair-dialogue.md` and `cocoder/decisions/0037-founder-stop-hold-resume.md` for the heading/section shape, and `cocoder/decisions/0014-living-adrs.md` for the living-ADR + founder-approval + conflict-audit convention you must honor.

STATUS LINE (mandatory): `**Status:** Proposed (pending founder approval)` — do NOT write 'Accepted' and do NOT invent a founder-approval date. The founder approves separately; this atom only drafts.

HEADERS: Seam = Oz control-plane autonomy — the write side. `**Amends:**` ADR-0016 (Deb scoped repair is idle-only/scoped), ADR-0017 (Oz bounded tool surface; 'read facts from disk, don't burn model calls'), ADR-0025 (atomic authoring cannot fabricate the founder-approved id/title/Objective). `**Relates to:**` ADR-0010 (founder-owned Objective gate), ADR-0023 (workspace commit spine), ADR-0036 (Oscar↔Deb repair dialogue). Read each amended/related ADR first so Context and the amendments are accurate, not guessed.

The ADR MUST decide exactly these things, sourced from the `oz-autonomy` priority Objective in `cocoder/priorities/oz-autonomy.md` (read it):
1. CONVERSATIONAL AUTHORING: Oz may draft a priority id/title/Objective with the founder in dashboard chat and commit it through the existing `author` spine on the founder's explicit go-ahead — no adhoc run launched. This amends ADR-0025's constraint.
2. SELF-DIRECTED REVERSIBLE GOVERNANCE EDITS without per-action approval: Oz may reorder priorities, open/close tickets, make narrow documentation fixes, and edit EXISTING priorities in ways that do NOT change a founder-approved Objective. This amends ADR-0016/0017.
3. THE `oz-action` GATE-COMMIT LANE: the daemon gate-commits only allowed paths as an `oz-action` commit and HOLDS BACK everything else (not silently committed, not silently dropped) — receipt shows landed vs held-back paths. Define the allowed-path set precisely.
4. THE FOUNDER GATE THAT STAYS (ADR-0010 preserved): a brand-NEW priority's Objective is committed ONLY on explicit founder approval; Oz may draft+surface but never fabricate/auto-commit a net-new Objective.
5. HARD EXCLUSIONS, forbidden even in self-direct mode: product/target code (`packages/*/src/`), secrets, install-local state, and all process/window/daemon lifecycle (F20/teardown unchanged). State teardown/stop semantics are NOT changed by this ADR.

CARRY-FORWARD POINTERS: do NOT edit ADR-0016/0017/0025 in this atom (they are Accepted; we don't point them at an unapproved ADR). Instead add a section titled exactly `## Carry-forward pointers (apply on approval)` that lists the precise one-line pointer edit to add to each of ADR-0016, ADR-0017, ADR-0025 once the founder approves 0040 — so a follow-up atom can apply them mechanically.

Include a brief `## Conflict audit` section (per ADR-0014's briefing requirement): how this interacts with ADR-0010 (founder Objective gate preserved), ADR-0023 (rides the one commit spine; no second commit lane), ADR-0036 (distinct from Oscar↔Deb repair), and ADR-0016/0017 (what specifically loosens). This is a judgment briefing, not a validator.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: (a) file exists at the path with `Status: Proposed (pending founder approval)`; (b) all five decisions above present and specific (allowed-path set and exclusions are concrete, not hand-wavy); (c) Amends/Relates-to headers present and accurate to the real ADR contents; (d) the carry-forward-pointers section names the exact edit for each of 0016/0017/0025; (e) a conflict-audit section is present. MUST NOT: edit ADR-0016/0017/0025; touch any product code, any test, or any file outside `cocoder/decisions/0040-oz-write-side-autonomy.md`; mark the ADR Accepted. Report the file path and a 3-line summary of the decisions when done.
